mapping.a_element: Cliffordization as a Hopf algebraic identity
mapping.b_element: Rota cliffordizations in symmetric function context

mapping.a_element: Hopf algebraic structure enabling Wick normal-ordering
mapping.b_element: Hopf and bi-algebraic structures underlying symmetric functions

mapping.a_element: closed algebraic expressions from Hopf methods
mapping.b_element: explicit presentation of symmetric function operations via Hopf structures

brief_justification: Both papers employ the same underlying Hopf algebraic machinery—specifically Rota cliffordization (twisted products)—to achieve combinatorial reordering: Wick normal-ordering in quantum field theory (A) and symmetric function operations (B). The abstracts explicitly link the method to Hopf algebras and cite Rota's framework as the shared mathematical core.
